rule: Tables can be created manually by defining columns and rows or automatically generated from collections of objects (POCOs).
Table output and appearance are customizable through predefined visual styles—including Default, Alternative, and Minimal—and configuration settings such as column alignment.
Table columns must be defined before adding rows, and the number of values in each row must match the number of defined columns.
Cell content exceeding the MaxWidth is automatically wrapped into sub-rows, using WordBreakDelimiters where possible to prevent mid-word splits.
Visual width and table alignment calculations must account for Unicode character widths.
Tables can be populated from hierarchical dictionary data structures where outer keys represent row labels.
Table output supports mixed data types and correctly handles multi-byte/Unicode character widths.
Table display and formatting options, such as numeric column alignment and row count footer visibility, are configurable.
Table formatting and layout support Unicode character widths, configurable cell alignment, and multiple output styles such as Markdown and standard grids.
Table output is directed to the standard system console by default.
Tables can be populated from various data sources including Lists, Dictionaries, DataTables, and Object Arrays.
Table structure requires columns to be defined before rows are added, and row data must match the established column count.
